Remember
A

Everything you've noticed, week by week.

Recap
A

A quiet look back at your week.

Week of July 6–12

This week carried a quiet undertone of comparison — several entries returned to measuring this moment against another time. Alongside that, connection kept surfacing as what actually lifted you: unplanned conversations, presence without agenda. Your projects leaned toward small, protective choices rather than ambitious ones.

Hi — what lifted

Unplanned time with people. Moments with no agenda kept showing up as the highlight, more than anything planned.

Lo — what weighed

A recurring comparison to an earlier, easier stretch of time. It showed up on three of five days.

Project — what moved forward

Small boundary-setting: leaving the phone behind, ending the day earlier, protecting one hour.

So, what now?
⌄

No advice here — just a couple of things worth sitting with.

— The comparison to last month showed up three separate days. What's it actually being measured against?

— Every "Hi" this week was unplanned. Is there a way to leave more room for that on purpose?

Past weeks
June 29 – July 5Read
June 22 – 28Read

Closing a chapter
Settling into the new job

28 days · 19 reflections

Your why, then

"So I can be present with my family this year, instead of carrying work home with me."

The season, at a glance
June 15July 12
← Back
Season Recap

Across four weeks, the throughline was recalibration — learning the shape of a new role while protecting space at home. Highs clustered around small wins at work and unplanned family time. Lows were quietest in week one and grew more present by week three, mostly around pace and comparison. Your projects consistently favored boundaries over ambition — a season of holding the line, more than pushing forward.

This chapter is complete.

You can begin the next one whenever you're ready — there's no rush.
